nodes.label: AI as collaborative thinking partner
nodes.label_negative: AI as unreliable or incorrect arguer
nodes.grounding_span: AI was my reminder, second opinion, fact checker, paper finder, and 'spot the difference science addition' all in one

nodes.label: AI reliability in fundamental knowledge
nodes.label_negative: AI getting basics wrong and arguing
nodes.grounding_span: sometimes the AI would give such incorrect answers or get things so far wrong on a fundamental 'GCSE basics' level, and then argue with me

nodes.label: AI as efficient literature search tool
nodes.grounding_span: The most helpful role for the AI assistant was definitely finding research papers

nodes.label: AI as safe space for brainstorming
nodes.label_negative: Fear of judgment in academic brainstorming
nodes.grounding_span: It was also nice to be able to say the most stupid, idiotic ideas and critique them legitimately, without any humiliation

nodes.label: Human-AI complementary partnership
nodes.grounding_span: AI and humans can not and should not replace each other. Instead, we need to go hand in hand

nodes.label: AI as lab partner and organizer
nodes.grounding_span: A lab partner would be fun... someone to talk to as I worked – that could take down all of my thoughts as I spoke them and then summarise them neatly

nodes.label: Scientific discovery and excitement
nodes.grounding_span: Science for me has always been about fun... making things explode, change colours, make people go 'wow'

nodes.label: Intellectual validation and growth
nodes.grounding_span: It was both refreshing and infuriating. Whether it agreed with me or not, the AI would have wonderful insight and allow me to consider things I may have not thought of

nodes.label: Efficiency and productivity in research
nodes.grounding_span: It was far more effecient at finding relevant material

nodes.label: Collaborative synergy and mutual improvement
nodes.grounding_span: We need to walk the path to the future together, where one improves the other and vice versa on a continuous cycle

nodes.label: Positive appreciation for AI as collaborator
nodes.grounding_span: AI was my reminder, second opinion, fact checker, paper finder, and 'spot the difference science addition' all in one

nodes.label: Frustration with AI's fundamental errors
nodes.grounding_span: sometimes the AI would give such incorrect answers or get things so far wrong on a fundamental 'GCSE basics' level, and then argue with me

nodes.label: Enthusiasm for AI as literature tool
nodes.grounding_span: The most helpful role for the AI assistant was definitely finding research papers

nodes.label: Desire for AI as lab partner and organizer
nodes.grounding_span: A lab partner would be fun... someone to talk to as I worked

nodes.label: Probing questioning to debug AI reasoning
nodes.grounding_span: I am a trained teacher so I can ask questions in such a way that I could get to the root of why the AI kicked out an incorrect answer

nodes.label: Exploratory and excitement-driven scientific approach
nodes.grounding_span: Science for me has always been about fun... making things explode, change colours, make people go 'wow'
